title: PaymentSession uses DateTime<Utc> — violates SQLite datetime invariant on future DB storage
description:
Scenario: nie-neg (Payment session persistence in wallet DB) is implemented. The PaymentSession struct is serialized into a SQLite column. chrono::DateTime<Utc> serializes via serde to RFC 3339 format: '2026-04-17T12:00:00Z'. CLAUDE.md §9 (SQLite datetime invariant) requires SQLite-compatible format: '2026-04-17 12:00:00'. An RFC 3339 string stored in a datetime column causes silently incorrect comparisons (e.g. 'expires_at > datetime("now")' fails because the T and Z characters sort differently than a space).

Maintenance consequence: nie-neg lands, session queries give wrong results, payments appear expired or stuck in wrong states, hard to diagnose because the values look correct when printed.

Fix now (before nie-neg): change created_at and updated_at field types. Either: (a) store as i64 unix timestamp (no format ambiguity, easy SQLite comparison), or (b) add a note to nie-neg to use a custom serde deserializer that reformats to the canonical SQLite format. Option (a) is simpler.
